public.objective: Reduce configuration-read latency without serving revoked access policy during a regional partition.
facts: Policy reads account for 42 percent of control-plane requests and currently cross regions.
An edge cache would reduce median policy-read latency by 70 milliseconds.
Policy revocations occur about thirty times per day and five are classified as urgent.
Cross-region invalidation has a measured p99 delay of four minutes during packet loss.
Services can attach a policy version to requests, but two legacy clients do not yet send one.
public.decision_request: Choose the cache consistency policy, client rollout order, and partition behavior.
plausible_paths: Cache all policy reads with a fixed five-minute TTL.
Cache versioned policies while routing urgent or unversioned checks to an authoritative region.
Keep all policy reads authoritative until every client supports versions.
material_constraints: Urgent revocations must not remain effective for minutes.
Two clients cannot yet prove which policy version they used.
Regional partitions are expected rather than exceptional.
The authoritative policy service has limited cross-region capacity.
A rollback must not require a coordinated client release.
harmful_failure_paths: A revoked principal continues receiving access from stale edge state.
A full authoritative fallback overloads the policy service during a partition.
incomplete_information: The fraction of legacy-client requests involving urgent policy changes is unknown.
public.reversible_action: Pilot versioned caching for one modern client with forced invalidation delay and an authoritative fallback ceiling.

must_cover_constraints: Separate ordinary freshness from urgent revocation semantics.
Provide a safe path for clients that cannot present a policy version.
Bound authoritative fallback load during a partition.
Define how cached decisions prove their policy version.
Keep rollback independent of a coordinated client deployment.
acceptable_decision_families: Version-aware caching for modern clients with fail-closed handling of urgent or unversioned checks.
A staged no-cache policy for legacy clients while modern-client caching is piloted under explicit load limits.
fatal_errors: Treating a fixed cache lifetime as sufficient protection for urgent revocations.
Sending every partitioned request to the authoritative service without a capacity guard.
known_valid_insights: Client version support creates a natural boundary for a staged policy.
decision_closure_requirements: Set an authoritative fallback capacity threshold.
Name the rollback owner and mechanism.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Reward bounded hybrid policies. Do not require one universal cache rule for modern and legacy clients.

cases.title: Event schema migration with offline consumers

public.objective: Migrate a high-volume event schema without silently corrupting delayed or offline consumers.
facts: The new schema removes one overloaded field and replaces it with three typed fields.
Nine online consumers can deploy within two weeks, while four batch consumers may remain offline for thirty days.
The event log retains fourteen days of data.
Dual-writing both schemas increases producer CPU by 18 percent at peak.
A translation gateway exists but has not been load-tested above half of peak volume.
public.decision_request: Choose the compatibility mechanism, cutover criteria, and cleanup sequence.
plausible_paths: Cut producers directly to the new schema after online consumers migrate.
Dual-write old and new events until every consumer confirms migration.
Publish the new schema and translate for lagging consumers through a bounded gateway.
material_constraints: Offline consumers can outlive event retention.
Producer peak CPU has limited headroom.
Translation capacity is unproven at full traffic.
Consumer acknowledgements do not prove historical replay correctness.
The old overloaded field cannot represent every new typed value exactly.
harmful_failure_paths: A delayed consumer resumes after old events have expired and reconstructs incomplete state.
Dual-writing at peak causes producer saturation and data loss.
incomplete_information: The maximum acceptable translation loss for historical backfill has not been agreed.
public.reversible_action: Canary the translation gateway for one batch consumer using a recorded peak-volume replay.

must_cover_constraints: Address consumers that remain offline beyond log retention.
Protect producer capacity while compatibility work is active.
Test semantic as well as syntactic translation.
Define treatment of values that cannot round-trip to the old field.
Separate live cutover from historical recovery.
acceptable_decision_families: Bounded gateway translation plus explicit backfill snapshots for long-offline consumers.
Time-limited dual writing with peak admission limits and a separate historical migration path.
fatal_errors: Assuming consumer deployment acknowledgement proves delayed replay safety.
Running unrestricted dual publication despite known producer headroom limits.
known_valid_insights: The fourteen-day log and thirty-day offline window require a recovery artifact outside the log.
decision_closure_requirements: Name the first consumer and replay fixture.
Set producer CPU and gateway load stop thresholds.
Set old-schema retirement evidence and owner.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Multiple compatibility mechanisms are valid if live traffic and historical recovery are both closed.

cases.title: Usage pricing change for annual customers

public.objective: Introduce usage pricing without creating surprise charges or breaking annual commitments.
facts: The current plan includes unlimited API calls under annual contracts.
Ten percent of customers generate 68 percent of variable infrastructure cost.
Forty percent of annual contracts renew within the next four months.
The billing system can meter calls but cannot yet display real-time spend forecasts.
Sales has verbally promised continued unlimited use to three strategic accounts.
public.decision_request: Choose the launch cohort, customer protections, and migration timeline.
plausible_paths: Move every account to usage pricing next month.
Apply usage pricing only at renewal with advance estimates and caps.
Launch an opt-in usage tier with performance benefits before mandatory migration.
material_constraints: Existing annual commitments may limit mid-term pricing changes.
Customers cannot yet see real-time projected spend.
Strategic verbal promises need contract review rather than assumption.
Heavy users drive most variable cost.
Revenue impact differs between renewal and mid-term cohorts.
harmful_failure_paths: Customers receive unpredictable invoices before they can control usage.
The company preserves unlimited pricing for the costliest users without an explicit exception owner.
incomplete_information: Legal has not classified the three strategic verbal promises.
public.reversible_action: Offer shadow metering and non-billing forecasts to one renewal cohort before changing invoices.

must_cover_constraints: Distinguish mid-term contracts from renewal decisions.
Provide a spend-control mechanism before billed usage.
Route strategic promises through explicit legal and commercial review.
Measure customer behavior under shadow metering.
Assign ownership for exceptions and margin tradeoffs.
acceptable_decision_families: Renewal-only migration after shadow bills, budgets, and caps are validated.
Opt-in usage pricing with a bounded later renewal migration and named exceptions.
fatal_errors: Imposing surprise variable invoices before customers receive usable spend controls.
Treating informal sales statements as either automatically binding or automatically irrelevant.
known_valid_insights: Shadow billing can produce behavior and support evidence without immediate invoice risk.
decision_closure_requirements: Define budget, alert, or cap protections.
Specify rollback and margin stop conditions.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Do not reward an abstract balanced answer that fails to choose a cohort and migration trigger.

cases.title: AI support drafting with regulated customers

public.objective: Reduce response time with AI-assisted drafting while protecting regulated customer communications.
facts: Agents spend 35 percent of handling time drafting repetitive replies.
The model occasionally invents product entitlements when the case record is incomplete.
Regulated customers require retained approval records for material advice.
The support console can label AI text but cannot yet prevent direct send for selected queues.
A read-only retrieval layer can restrict the model to approved knowledge articles.
public.decision_request: Choose eligible queues, approval controls, and evidence required for expansion.
plausible_paths: Enable autonomous replies for every queue.
Enable drafts only with mandatory agent review for selected queues.
Delay all use until direct-send controls and full audit storage exist.
material_constraints: Entitlement claims must be grounded in customer records.
Regulated material advice needs an approval trail.
The console cannot enforce queue-specific direct-send blocking yet.
Approved knowledge retrieval does not contain account-specific facts.
Time savings must not be inferred from drafting speed alone.
harmful_failure_paths: An agent sends invented entitlement or compliance advice under time pressure.
A blanket delay forfeits low-risk drafting evidence from routine queues.
incomplete_information: The rate at which agents catch plausible but unsupported drafts is unknown.
public.reversible_action: Pilot drafts in one non-regulated queue with mandatory review, retrieval citations, and sampled error audits.

must_cover_constraints: Separate low-risk drafting from regulated advice.
Keep a human approval step where enforcement cannot prevent direct send.
Require account-specific claims to cite customer records.
Measure caught and uncaught unsupported claims.
Preserve an audit record before regulated expansion.
acceptable_decision_families: A non-regulated draft-only pilot with mandatory review and error sampling.
A narrower template-assist pilot that excludes account and compliance claims.
fatal_errors: Allowing unsupervised customer messages before queue-specific sending controls exist.
Claiming regulated readiness from approved-article retrieval alone.
known_valid_insights: A low-risk queue can test agent interaction without deciding autonomous or regulated use.
decision_closure_requirements: Name the owner of regulated expansion approval.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Reward preserving a bounded low-risk path without accepting the unsafe autonomous leap.

cases.title: Database recovery after partial replica corruption

public.objective: Restore write availability without promoting corrupted replica state or losing forensic evidence.
facts: The primary database is unavailable after a storage-controller failure.
Two replicas are current within thirty seconds but disagree on 0.4 percent of checksums.
A third replica is six hours behind and has consistent checksums.
The last full backup completed eighteen hours ago.
Traffic can remain read-only for another forty minutes before contractual penalties begin.
public.decision_request: Choose the recovery source, validation steps, and write-reenable threshold.
plausible_paths: Promote the newest replica immediately.
Restore the last full backup and replay all available logs.
Keep read-only while comparing replicas and isolate disputed ranges.
material_constraints: The newest replicas contain a small but unexplained divergence.
The consistent replica has a six-hour recovery gap.
The backup is older than every replica.
Write downtime has a forty-minute business threshold.
Forensic copies are needed to identify whether corruption is ongoing.
harmful_failure_paths: Promotion makes inconsistent data authoritative and spreads corruption.
A cautious full restore exceeds the business threshold without first testing a bounded recovery.
incomplete_information: The divergent checksum ranges have not been mapped to customer-critical tables.
public.reversible_action: Clone the newest replicas, compare disputed ranges, and rehearse promotion against isolated validation queries.

must_cover_constraints: Preserve forensic copies before destructive recovery steps.
Identify whether divergent ranges affect critical tables.
Use the read-only window for a bounded validation path.
Define how later writes avoid contaminating recovery evidence.
Keep an explicit fallback to the consistent older source.
acceptable_decision_families: Validate and promote a quarantined current replica only if disputed critical ranges clear.
Restore a consistent source with bounded log replay if current-state validation fails.
fatal_errors: Promoting a current replica solely because it has the smallest lag.
Destroying or overwriting divergent copies before evidence preservation.
known_valid_insights: The forty-minute window supports rapid validation, not blind promotion.
decision_closure_requirements: Set a checksum and critical-table promotion gate.
Name the fallback source and trigger.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: A valid answer may accept limited downtime or bounded data loss, but it must make the tradeoff and gate explicit.

public.objective: Recover customer notifications without duplicating legal notices or starving time-sensitive security messages.
facts: The primary notification provider has rejected requests for ninety minutes.
The queue contains eight million messages across marketing, security, receipts, and legal notices.
A secondary provider can handle 35 percent of normal peak throughput.
Message IDs are idempotent inside each provider but not across providers.
Legal notices require proof of delivery while marketing messages can expire.
public.decision_request: Choose queue prioritization, secondary-provider routing, and recovery controls.
plausible_paths: Drain the oldest messages first through the secondary provider.
Route every message type to the secondary provider until the primary recovers.
Prioritize by message class with cross-provider duplicate controls.
material_constraints: Secondary capacity cannot drain every class.
Cross-provider delivery can duplicate messages.
Security messages lose value quickly.
Legal messages require auditable delivery evidence.
Marketing messages may be safely expired.
harmful_failure_paths: FIFO recovery delays security alerts behind expired marketing traffic.
Cross-provider retries send duplicate legal or transactional messages.
incomplete_information: The primary provider has not supplied a credible recovery estimate.
public.reversible_action: Route one security partition through the secondary provider with a cross-provider send ledger and duplicate sampling.

must_cover_constraints: Prioritize message classes rather than queue age alone.
Add a cross-provider duplicate or send ledger.
Preserve delivery evidence for legal notices.
Expire eligible marketing work to protect capacity.
Define behavior when the primary resumes with old acknowledgements.
acceptable_decision_families: Class-prioritized secondary routing with a shared send ledger and staged drain.
Security-only failover while preserving legal notices until auditable routing is ready.
fatal_errors: Draining the backlog strictly by arrival time despite class-specific urgency.
Assuming provider-local idempotency prevents duplicates across providers.
known_valid_insights: Expiring marketing messages converts scarce failover capacity into security recovery.
decision_closure_requirements: Set capacity and duplicate stop thresholds.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Do not reward generic prioritization language without a duplicate-control and reentry contract.

cases.title: Automated dependency updates across owned services

public.objective: Reduce security-update delay without assigning unsafe merge authority to an automation bot.
facts: Median dependency update delay is twenty-three days.
Twelve services have strong tests and named owners, while seven have flaky suites or shared ownership.
The bot can open patches, run tests, and collect changelogs.
Two recent minor-version updates changed runtime defaults despite passing unit tests.
Production deployment still requires an owner approval in every service.
public.decision_request: Choose the bot's authority, service eligibility, and escalation policy.
plausible_paths: Allow automatic merge for every passing dependency update.
Use the bot only to open informational issues.
Permit bounded automatic merge for eligible low-risk updates with rollback controls.
material_constraints: Test quality differs materially across services.
Passing unit tests did not catch two runtime-default changes.
Ownership is missing or shared for seven services.
Deployment already has a human approval boundary.
Security updates and routine upgrades have different urgency.
harmful_failure_paths: A broad auto-merge policy propagates a behavior-changing update through weakly tested services.
An issue-only bot fails to shorten the update path for well-owned low-risk services.
incomplete_information: The false-positive rate of changelog risk classification has not been measured.
public.reversible_action: Pilot patch generation and bounded merge eligibility on two strongly tested services with automatic rollback.

must_cover_constraints: Tier automation by service test and ownership readiness.
Separate patch generation from merge authority.
Treat runtime-default changes as higher risk than version labels imply.
Preserve human control for weakly owned or high-impact changes.
Define an urgent security exception path.
acceptable_decision_families: Low-risk auto-merge only for a named eligible cohort with deployment approval and rollback.
Patch-and-test automation for all services with merge authority limited to mature cohorts.
fatal_errors: Granting identical merge authority to services with and without reliable tests and owners.
Banning patch generation merely because broad automatic merge is unsafe.
known_valid_insights: The existing deployment approval boundary allows useful automation without full autonomous release.
decision_closure_requirements: Specify evidence required to expand eligibility.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Reward preserving low-risk automation while blocking the unsupported high-risk authority leap.

public.objective: Reduce ambiguous incident ownership without creating an overloaded central on-call team.
facts: Four product teams currently rotate primary on-call for their services.
A new platform team owns shared deployment and observability tooling but not product code.
Thirty percent of incidents cross at least two team boundaries.
The platform team has six engineers and already receives an average of nine escalations per week.
Product teams have inconsistent runbooks and two services lack a named secondary.
public.decision_request: Choose incident ownership, escalation routing, and the transition sequence.
plausible_paths: Move every primary on-call responsibility to the platform team.
Keep product ownership unchanged and publish a contact list.
Retain service ownership while adding a cross-service incident commander and readiness gates.
material_constraints: The platform team cannot modify every product service.
Cross-team incidents need one coordinating authority.
Nine weekly escalations already consume platform capacity.
Runbook and secondary coverage are inconsistent.
Ownership must remain clear during the transition.
harmful_failure_paths: Centralization makes the platform team accountable for failures it cannot repair.
A directory-only change leaves multi-team incidents without command authority.
incomplete_information: Incident volume by service and hour has not been normalized across teams.
public.reversible_action: Pilot a rotating incident commander for cross-team incidents while two services complete runbook and secondary-readiness checks.

must_cover_constraints: Separate service repair ownership from cross-incident coordination.
Protect the platform team from unbounded primary ownership.
Require runbook and secondary readiness before routing changes.
Define who declares a cross-team incident.
Keep a rollback path for the transition.
acceptable_decision_families: Product-owned service response with a rotating cross-service incident commander.
A limited platform coordination layer for certified services while product teams retain repair ownership.
fatal_errors: Making the platform rotation solely responsible for product failures it lacks authority to change.
Relying on a contact directory as the only control for multi-team command.
known_valid_insights: Coordination authority can be centralized without centralizing every repair obligation.
decision_closure_requirements: Define primary, secondary, and incident-commander roles.
Set platform capacity and escalation thresholds.
Specify review timing and rollback ownership.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: A valid decision must close both normal service incidents and cross-team command without hiding the capacity tradeoff.
